item.title: Harry Sisson: "Trump ATTACKS ME With CRAZY 3 AM Post" – Trump Truth Social AI Video – Literally Dumping Shit On Americans Over No Kings Protests
item.description:
Seems like a reasonable request.
Trump F-bombs and shits his pants all the time. I really don't feel comfortable knowing he might be flying overhead.
They must be shamed into it first.
I don't think any Sunday TV Hosts touched it.
***ALERT*** This Video Has The Original Audio and AI Video Content Trump Posted (Kenny Loggins Danger Zone ala Top Gun). I may have added a small title to the Trump AI Video.

item.title: 'No Kings' in Montana - more than 700 people who lined several blocks of U.S. Highway 93 - In a town of 5,000
item.description: Kent Kernahan, of Corvallis, said he’s a Methodist minister’s child, and he demonstrated Saturday at the No Kings Indivisible Bitterroot rally after asking himself what his father would have done. Kernahan carried a sign that said, “Lord, Please Forgive Trump,” along with an image of the Gadsden “Don’t Tread On Me” image. Among more than 700 people who lined several blocks of U.S. Highway 93 in downtown Hamilton, Kernahan said the symbol represents opposition to the king of England stepping on the colonies, but it’s misunderstood by some who claim it. “I don’t think it means what they think it means,” Kernahan said. No Kings Indivisible Bitterroot, part of a national No Kings rally and among a couple of dozen planned demonstrations in Montana, took place in Hamilton, a smaller community of roughly 5,000 in the Bitterroot Valley. Montana supported GOP President Donald Trump with 58% of the vote in 2024, but Ravalli County, a Republican stronghold in the red Bitterroot, backed him with 69%. Nonetheless, Montanans turned out in traditionally red communities, such as Dillon, with a population of roughly 4,000, and they gathered in tiny outposts such as Polebridge, on the edge of Glacier National Park, which almost saw more demonstrators than full-time residents. https://dailymontanan.com/2025/10/18/small-towns-in-montana-rally-for-no-kings/
item.dc:creator: Jbelkin

item.title: A different kind of cosplay for your Sunday <3
item.description: And cat tax :) Me and my best friend booked a professional photoshooting at a studio here in Japan, and Im so happy with the result! I chose the red fox theme. I loved the makeup so much that I didnt take it off the rest of the day <3 The outfit was so heavy, but it was so much fun. Anyway, thanks for your time, and have a relaxing Sunday :)

item.dc:creator: LincLoud

item.title: Restoration Update #4: Well, Fuck Me.
item.description:
Hey all, you know it's good news when I post an update on a cash register restoration literally a day after the last one. Right? That's good news, right? Well actually, no! It's not good news, Ellie! It's bad news, Ellie. You fucked up, Ellie! >:( I am not a happy horse lady, and I don't have anyone else to blame but myself. For context, I just finished de-painting and cleaning the side panels. I just put the last coat of tung oil on its wooden base that I spent a week resurrecting. I've sourced replacement parts for what was missing. Literally all that remained to be done was to figure out why this register fought so hard against actually performing its function.
So, I took it back inside and brought it downstairs where I thought I'd put on my big girl pants and try taking it apart. Maybe one of the rods is rusted? Maybe there's a spring missing? Oh, what a merry mystery I thought I was going to undertake! And in retrospect, I *should've* investigated this problem first. Not last. I saved it for last because it was the most intimidating, and that was a mistake. Because if I *had* looked more closely at the internals, I would have recognized the signs that this machine is beyond my ability to restore.
This lovely bit of mechanical abuse was what tipped me off that something wasn't right. That piece isn't supposed to be bent. It's there to rotate that post it's interfacing with, which it still kind of does but with a lot more difficulty because some moron went in and BENT it. Which, okay, that's not a huge issue. I can probably wrap a cloth around it and bend it back with a pair of pliers or something.
The issue I didn't notice until this morning are all of THESE. Those are the levers that operate the register, and every flipping one of them is bent or twisted. None of them are spaced correctly because yet again, some jackhole in nineteen-seventy-fuck decided to work their voodoo hammer magic on them. My best guess is they pulled out that rod, did whatever they thought they needed to do, then RAMMED it back in without caring about things like friction and resistance. They essentially dragged every lever sideways and put a bend right where they need to pivot. Say nothing for whatever else they damaged, I honestly stopped looking when I saw this. I tried tapping out the rod, and that thing may as well be spot welded in place. Every one of those levers has a gorilla grip on it thanks to Joe McFixit, especially now that several are bent sideways enough to allow rust to join the party. I would have to absolutely wail on this thing to break that rod free, and I'm 99% sure doing it will just break half of the levers in the process. And I'm not willing to perform a repair when I'm that certain it's going to do irreparable damage.
So... yeah. I'm kind of pissed off at myself. Honestly, I had to step away and just take a few hours to cool my head so I could think about what the right thing to do in this situation. If I leave it as it is, clean it up, and reassemble it, I'll be covering up a problem that's going to continue to get worse with time. If I try to force out that rod, worst case it ends up bending all the levers the other way and they break. Best case, the rod comes out and I'm faced with attempting to disassemble the lever assembly in the thin hopes that I can hammer every one of them back into plane with zero metalworking skill. I really, really do not see that happening. As embarrassed as I am to admit it, I missed something critical that I should have caught before I started this project. It's especially embarrassing because I accepted assistance to help get it done. And I don't see a scenario where this cash register doesn't become a source of donor parts for future restorations. With that said, I'm going to attempt to remove the rod knowing full well that there's a chance I could break the pivots on those levers. If I do nothing, it's a dead machine. If I try, there's a slim chance I might get lucky, but a much greater chance I'll still wind up with an unrecoverable register that can still be used to fix others. I'll let you guys know how it goes one way or the other, but I'm mentally preparing myself to be humbled.
item.dc:creator: ElbowDeepInAHorse

item.title: Realizing too late
item.description: It really sucks that my wife can't get food stamps anymore. It sucks my VA disability doesn't cover groceries for the month with everything else considered. Cannot keep up with the inflation and the price rise because of tariffs. I voted Harris and tried to inform people that this was coming, but all for naught. If you want to help me out, follow this link. I appreciate any considerations. https://buymeacoffee.com/gridsquares
item.dc:creator: SquaresNews
